utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Marcus, how have you been doing on the higher dose of bupropion?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: Energy is better. Mornings used to take me an hour to get going, now maybe fifteen minutes.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Any side effects? Sleep, appetite, headaches?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: A little harder to fall asleep, but I'm taking it earlier in the day now. No headaches.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Any signs of elevated mood, racing thoughts, irritability?

utterances.speaker: PATIENT
utterances.text: No. Mood feels stable. PHQ-9 I filled out this morning was an eight.

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Eight is a real improvement. Are you tolerating the lamotrigine 200mg?

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Okay. Plan: continue lamotrigine 200mg daily, continue bupropion XL 300mg daily. We'll check labs in three months. Any questions?

utterances.speaker: CLINICIAN
utterances.text: Sending 90-day supply for both to your pharmacy now. See you in six weeks.

patient.name: Marcus Lee
diagnoses: F31.81 Bipolar II disorder, current depressed

goals: Stabilize mood (no hypomanic episodes for 90 days)

patient.previous_session_summary: Tolerating lamotrigine well, no rash. Reported residual fatigue. Increased bupropion XL from 150mg to 300mg two weeks ago.
